title: Kanbus card palette not using Radix tokens
description:
Observed:
React Storybook Kanbus card palette uses hardcoded hex values (non-Radix).

Expected:
Palette should be sourced from Radix dark color tokens for consistency with Python/radix usage.

Impact:
Harder to maintain color consistency and match device palette expectations.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting fix: align Kanbus clock demo colors to Radix dark tokens. Will audit React Storybook components + Python fallback to ensure all colors come from Radix palette; also confirm clock face palette source.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented Radix dark token mapping in Storybook and switched KanbusCard + Clock stories to use Radix tokens (no hardcoded hex). Python kanbus_clock now uses discrete Radix steps (band7/8/9/11 + band2/4/5) instead of lighten/darken. Next: verify visuals on device.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Info screens now use Radix dark tokens on both Python (kanbus_clock) and React Storybook components. If comment should be the only bright element, we need to dim header/issue/parent text relative to comment; current palette uses band8/7 for header/top and sand11 for comment.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updated palette to use explicit Radix steps: base=7, attention=9, dim=6 (dark scale). Header/parent/issue now base; comment + transition body + transition issue-type/status use attention. React KanbusCard mirrors same mapping via radixColors.ts.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Aligned backgrounds: KanbusCard now uses a single body background for all non-header rows (header bg separate). Parent/issue/bottom rows no longer use headerBg or parent band background; only text color varies by type.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Bumped attention step to 10 (base=7, dim=6). Comment text now sand10; attention elements (transition/created body + status/issue-type in header) use step 10 in both Python and React.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Bumped attention step to 11 so comment text + transition/created attention elements use Radix dark step 11; base remains 7, dim 6.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Header background dimmed one Radix step: headerBg now band3 (was band4) in both Python and React Kanbus cards.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment text color switched to Radix dark sky (step 11) in both Python and React Kanbus cards.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Reverted header background to Radix band4 (one notch lighter again) for both Python + React Kanbus cards.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Parent row background now uses Radix band step 3 (header still step 4, body step 2). Applied in Python and React.
